name: Batter Fried Sage Leaves (Salbey Zu Backen)
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Melted butter that has been allowed to cool
recipeInstructions:
This recipe is taken almost verbatim from a cookbook originally published in 1709; the language has been slightly modernized, but only two ingredients have been added (sugar and salt).
plenty of nice fresh sage leaves oil or shortening for deep frying
Take the flour and mix with the cold water into a thick batter that must be smooth. Then mix in the beaten egg and stir well; pour in a little sour wine, yet not too much. Beat the egg white into soft peaks and fold into the batter. Finally, add the butter as well as a very small amount of salt and sugar and stir the batter once more. Wash and dry off the sage leaves, dredge them in the batter, and fry them on both sides in hot lard until crispy.
Note: I would suggest folding in the egg white last of all and letting the batter sit for about 30 minutes beforehand. Also, vegetable oil or shortening is recommended for deep frying rather than lard.
Makes 8 servings.
